twitter_thread: 27% of dividend stocks yielding 5% or more will cut payouts in the next 12 months, wiping out $1.4B in investor income
3M and Procter & Gamble have grown dividends 10%+ annually for 10+ years, outpacing inflation
Finviz screens show 14 stocks with 10-year dividend growth streaks, including Coca-Cola and Johnson & Johnson
Payout ratios above 80% and debt-to-equity ratios above 1.5 are red flags for dividend traps
Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF has returned 12.1% annually over 10 years, with 30% less volatility than the S&P 500
Energy and real estate stocks have betas of 1.2 and 1.1, respectively, making them risky dividend plays
What's your experience with dividend investing? Do you prioritize 4% yield or 10% annual growth? #passiveincome #dividends
pack.linkedin: 27% of dividend stocks yielding 5% or more will cut payouts in the next 12 months, a $1.4B loss for investors. To avoid this, focus on dividend growers like 3M and Procter & Gamble, with 10%+ annual dividend growth over 10+ years. Use Finviz to screen for similar stocks, and consider Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF for diversification. Be cautious of stocks with payout ratios above 80% and debt-to-equity ratios above 1.5. What's your approach to dividend investing - yield or growth?
